description: LinkedIn Automation Channel: Add LinkedIn as a channel with data models for profile/connection tracking, sequence step types (send_connection_request, send_linkedin_message, view_profile), LinkedIn message templates, and connection state management. Actual LinkedIn API is stubbed.
steps: Update packages/shared/src/types/index.ts: Add 'linkedin' to CHANNEL array. Add LINKEDIN_STEP_TYPE const array ('send_connection_request', 'send_linkedin_message', 'view_profile'). Add LINKEDIN_CONNECTION_STATUS ('not_connected', 'pending', 'connected', 'declined'). Add 'send_connection_request', 'send_linkedin_message', 'view_profile' to SEQUENCE_STEP_TYPE array.

Add Prisma models: `LinkedInProfile` (id, leadId:String @unique, profileUrl:String, headline?, connectionStatus:String @default('not_connected'), connectionRequestedAt?, connectedAt?, lastMessagedAt?, createdAt, updatedAt). Add `LinkedInMessage` (id, leadId, profileId, direction, body, status:String @default('pending'), sentAt?, createdAt). Add enum LinkedInConnectionStatus to schema.
Create Prisma migration for LinkedInProfile and LinkedInMessage models.

Create `apps/api/src/services/linkedin.ts`: LinkedIn service (stub implementation). Functions: `sendConnectionRequest(leadId, note?)` - updates profile status to 'pending', logs activity. `sendLinkedInMessage(leadId, body)` - creates LinkedInMessage record. `viewProfile(leadId)` - logs profile view activity. `getConnectionStatus(leadId)` - returns current status. `updateConnectionStatus(leadId, status)` - for webhook callbacks. All are stubs that work with local DB only.

Create `apps/api/src/routes/linkedin.ts`: POST /api/linkedin/connect/:leadId - send connection request. POST /api/linkedin/message/:leadId - send message. POST /api/linkedin/view/:leadId - view profile. GET /api/linkedin/profile/:leadId - get LinkedIn profile data. PUT /api/linkedin/profile/:leadId - update profile URL/status. GET /api/linkedin/connections - list all connections with status filter.

Integrate with sequence engine: Update `apps/api/src/services/sequence-engine.ts` to handle the new step types (send_connection_request, send_linkedin_message, view_profile). When these steps execute, call the linkedin service.
Update mockDb.ts: Add `linkedInProfile` and `linkedInMessage` mock models. Add `buildLinkedInProfile()` and `buildLinkedInMessage()` factories.
Create `apps/api/src/services/linkedin.test.ts`: Test connection request flow, message sending, profile view logging, status updates.
Create `apps/api/src/routes/linkedin.test.ts`: Test all LinkedIn route endpoints.
Register LinkedIn routes in apps/api/src/index.ts with requireAuth middleware.
Frontend: Create `apps/web/src/pages/LinkedIn.tsx`: Page showing LinkedIn connections, pending requests, message history.
Frontend: Add LinkedIn profile section to lead detail view with connection status badge and action buttons.
Update Sidebar to include LinkedIn under Channels section.
acceptance_criteria: LinkedIn channel is available in the CHANNEL type
LinkedIn sequence step types work in the sequence engine
POST /api/linkedin/connect/:leadId creates/updates LinkedInProfile with pending status
LinkedIn profile data persists per lead
Sequence engine correctly handles send_connection_request, send_linkedin_message, view_profile steps
findings: All 29 LinkedIn tests pass. Full API test suite (1174 tests) and web test suite (104 tests) pass. API TypeScript build has pre-existing errors in unrelated files (smtp-server.test.ts, api-keys.ts, demo.pipeline.test.ts, auth.ts, email-sandbox.ts, simulation.test.ts) that were present before this feature. Shared and Web packages build cleanly. Workers package also has pre-existing build errors.
